steps.title: Identify the parlor's dual demands
steps.content: The front parlor is Tia Ana's classroom space by day, but every evening it becomes Salomé's salon — and crucially, it is never in order for its transformation back to a classroom the following morning. Tia Ana checks repeatedly whether the guests have left, which reveals she needs the space back to prepare it.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
Her repeated checks show she needs the parlor cleared so she can ready it for class. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — 'She finds the guests to be dull and is eager to find a way to leave the salon.': Her concern is practical — the space must be reset — not boredom with the guests; no textual evidence supports this motive. ✗
  • Choice B — 'She is eager to tell the guests about the new school she is opening and anticipates their approval of her plans.': The passage describes her waiting for guests to leave, not planning to address them; this invents an unsupported motivation. ✗
  • Choice C — 'She is anxious for the gathering to disperse so that she can ready the space for her own needs.': This matches exactly: the parlor is never set back in order for the classroom, so she monitors when it will be free. ✓
  • Choice D — 'She is frustrated because she needs assistance elsewhere in the house, but Salomé is unavailable while entertaining the guests.': The passage focuses on the parlor itself, not on needing Salomé's help in another part of the house; this redirects to a separate issue not in the text. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
